title: Queries and mutations
summary: Queries read data; mutations change data. Both are sent to a single endpoint with a typed schema.
content:
## Queries

Ask for exactly the fields you need—no under or over-fetching by field selection.

## Mutations

Conventionally one mutation root field per operation. Return updated objects so clients can update caches.

## Errors

Partial success can include both `data` and `errors` per the GraphQL spec.

code_examples.title: Query
Mutation

title: Schema and resolvers
summary: The schema declares types and fields. Resolvers fetch or compute each field—often backed by databases or REST services.
content:
## Type system

Objects, interfaces, unions, enums, inputs, and custom scalars compose your API contract.

## Resolver chain

Each field can have its own resolver; parent object flows down the tree.
